prerequisites: An active Amazon Ads account with advertising data you want to sync.
Access to the Amazon Advertising API, which requires completing Amazon's API onboarding/approval process.
A Login with Amazon (LWA) security profile created in the Amazon Developer Console, providing a Client ID and Client Secret.
Your API region: North America (NA), Europe (EU), or Far East (FE), matching where your advertising profiles live.
A redirect URI registered on your LWA security profile, used during the authorization grant step that produces the Refresh Token.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (Login with Amazon)
authMethods.summary: The Amazon Advertising API authorizes via OAuth 2.0 through Login with Amazon (LWA). Supply the Client ID (client_id) and Client Secret (client_secret) from your LWA security profile, plus a long-lived Refresh Token (refresh_token) obtained by exchanging an authorization grant from an Amazon Ads account. The connector uses the Refresh Token to programmatically mint short-lived access tokens for each request.

setupSteps.title: Get access to the Amazon Advertising API
setupSteps.body: Apply for and complete Amazon's Advertising API onboarding for your Amazon Ads account. API access must be granted before you can authorize and pull data.

setupSteps.title: Create a Login with Amazon security profile
setupSteps.body: In the Amazon Developer Console, create a Login with Amazon (LWA) security profile for your application. Record the Client ID and Client Secret it generates, and add an allowed return/redirect URI to the profile for use during authorization.

setupSteps.title: Generate a Refresh Token
setupSteps.body: Use your LWA Client ID and Client Secret to run the OAuth 2.0 authorization grant flow against an Amazon Ads account. The redirect_uri you send must match one configured on the security profile. Exchange the returned authorization grant at the regional LWA token endpoint to obtain a Refresh Token (and access token).

setupSteps.title: Enter your OAuth credentials
setupSteps.body: Provide the required secret fields client_id, client_secret, and refresh_token. These are the LWA Client ID, Client Secret, and the Refresh Token from the previous step.

setupSteps.title: Set the region and sync scope
setupSteps.body: Set region to NA, EU, or FE to target the correct API endpoints. Optionally restrict the sync with profiles (specific advertising profile IDs) and/or marketplace_ids; a profile is selected if it matches either a given profile ID or marketplace ID. Leave both empty to sync all accessible profiles.

setupSteps.title: Configure the reporting window and throughput
setupSteps.body: Optionally set start_date (YYYY-MM-DD; defaults to yesterday in UTC) for the earliest report date, look_back_window (days to re-pull to capture late-arriving data; defaults to 3), and num_workers (number of concurrent request threads; lower it if you hit rate limits).
